message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes (extension scoring, lifecycle perf tests, beads)

Add interference matrix completeness reporting to extension_scoring.rs,
apply clippy cleanups to e2e_extension_lifecycle_perf.rs (let-else, if
reorder, f64::from), and sync bead state updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Observed bottleneck pattern:
- Session mutations trigger save behavior too frequently.
- Current save implementations incur excessive rewrite costs in both JSONL and SQLite paths.

Design intent:
- Decouple mutation from persistence via controlled batching.
- Introduce explicit durability modes to make tradeoffs transparent.
- Convert persistence operations to incremental append/upsert behavior where possible.
- Keep correctness first via crash-consistency testing.

Success condition:
- Material reduction in append/save latency on large-session workloads with no reliability regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:10:58Z"},{"id":1759,"issue_id":"bd-3ar8v.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Patched AutosaveQueue::finish_flush in src/session.rs to restore failed in-flight batches using remaining capacity (max_pending_mutations - pending_mutations) instead of full queue cap.  {"id":"bd-3ar8v.2.1","title":"[Phase 1] Remove per-append synchronous save behavior from session mutations","description":"Stop calling save() on every append path and separate mutation from persistence.","design":"Refactor session mutation APIs so append/change operations no longer invoke save inline. Introduce explicit persistence signaling to background pipeline.","acceptance_criteria":"1) No per-append direct save call remains in mutation path. 2) Functional behavior of session mutation APIs remains correct. 3) Unit tests cover mutation without immediate persistence side effects. 4) No regressions in session content/ordering semantics.","notes":"Reasoning: inline save coupling creates write amplification and high tail latency at large history sizes.

Tests cover:
1. Multi-phase append→crash→recover→continue lifecycle
2. Checkpoint healing via header dirty flag
3. Stale temp file isolation after interrupted rewrite
4. Autosave queue mutation tracking through faults
5. Durability mode behavior matrix (strict/balanced/throughput)
6. Rapid 10-cycle crash-recover stress test
7. Header dirty forces clean rewrite
8. V2 store segment corruption recovery
9. Read-only filesystem simulation
10. Mixed entry types through crash cycle
11. Corruption healed at checkpoint
12. Orphaned parent link detection
13. Save idempotency after recovery
14. Large session (100 entries) scattered corruption

Key design decisions:
- Uses public API only (no private field access from integration tests)
- TraceLog structured diagnostics system for debugging
- Forces full rewrites via header dirtying (set_model_header) instead of direct counter manipulation
- After recovery from corruption, healing save required before incremental appends work correctly","created_at":"2026-02-16T03:25:44Z"}]}

Background thread writes entire buffer in one write_all() call instead of N separate writes, reducing syscall overhead.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusCode","created_at":"2026-02-16T22:57:59.228755815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T22:59:05.838925170Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T22:59:05.838827889Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf-3x","phase-1","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ar8v.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-3ar8v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T22:57:59.228755815Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1890,"issue_id":"bd-3ar8v.2.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented and committed (555758c3). Changed incremental append path from Vec<Vec<u8>> to single Vec<u8> buffer. to_vec() per entry replaced with to_writer() into shared buffer.
